text: <par>are these New forms correctly tagged? cf. other notes on these forms.</par>
<par>Newar <latinform>duru</latinform> 'milk' is a loan from Skt. <emph>dugdha-</emph> (cf. <xref ref="3466">#3466</xref>); <latinform>ca</latinform> DIMINUTIVE.</par>

duru is Sanskrit for milk
-gu = one which is = relativizing/nominalizing suffix

<par>K. P. Malla (p.c. 2007) analyzes this form as consisting of <latinform>te</latinform> 'navel', <latinform>pu</latinform> 'seed, round thing', <latinform>ca</latinform> (preferably <latinform>chaa</latinform>) 'DIMINUTIVE; child'. Newar <latinform>chaa</latinform> appears in compounds like <latinform>ma-chaa</latinform> 'a child' and <latinform>khi-chaa</latinform> 'a dog'.</par>

<par>The voiceless Newar initial perhaps points to a variant with the <reconstruction>*s-</reconstruction> prefix. On the other hand, the Dolakha form suggests that this initial results from the fusion of a disyllabic prototype.</par>
<par>Borrowing from NP? Cf. Magar, et al.</par>
<par>According to K. P. Malla (p.c. 2007), Newar <latinform>-ku</latinform> serves as a classifier for "a piece with some magnitude in girth or depth".</par>
